name: circleci-automation
description: Automate CircleCI tasks via Rube MCP (Composio): trigger pipelines, monitor workflows/jobs, retrieve artifacts and test metadata. Always search tools first for current schemas.
capabilities: Trigger CircleCI pipelines on specific branches or tags with custom parameters
Monitor pipeline and workflow execution status in real time
Retrieve job details including executor type, parallelism, and execution times
Download build artifacts from completed jobs
Access test results and metadata including failure messages
Navigate paginated API responses for large result sets
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis detected 64 patterns but all are false positives. The SKILL.md file is Markdown documentation, not executable code. Backtick patterns are code formatting syntax, not Ruby execution. The network reference is a legitimate MCP endpoint configuration. No security concerns identified after manual review.
valueStatement: Streamline your CI/CD operations by triggering CircleCI pipelines, monitoring workflow status, and retrieving build artifacts through natural language. Eliminate manual dashboard navigation and accelerate your development feedback loop.
limitations: Requires active Rube MCP connection with CircleCI toolkit configured
Cannot execute jobs or modify CircleCI configuration files directly
Test metadata retrieval depends on jobs uploading JUnit XML format results
Artifact downloads may require additional authentication headers

useCases.targetUser: DevOps engineers managing continuous integration workflows
useCases.title: CI/CD Pipeline Management
useCases.description: Trigger new pipeline runs when code changes are pushed, monitor execution progress, and receive notifications when workflows complete or fail.

useCases.title: Build Debugging and Analysis
useCases.description: Investigate failed builds by retrieving job logs, examining test failure details, and downloading artifacts for local analysis.

useCases.title: Release Automation
useCases.description: Automate release pipeline triggering with specific parameters, track deployment workflows, and collect build artifacts for distribution.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Pipeline Trigger
promptTemplates.prompt: Trigger a CircleCI pipeline for the repository gh/myorg/myrepo on the main branch.

promptTemplates.title: Workflow Status Check
promptTemplates.prompt: Check the status of all workflows for the most recent pipeline of gh/myorg/myrepo and tell me which jobs have failed.

promptTemplates.title: Artifact Retrieval
promptTemplates.prompt: Get the list of artifacts produced by job number 123 in gh/myorg/myrepo and provide the download URLs for any PDF files.

promptTemplates.title: Test Results Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Retrieve test metadata for job 456 in gh/myorg/myrepo, summarize the test results, and list all failed tests with their error messages.

bestPractices: Always search for available tools using RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS before executing any CircleCI operations to ensure you have current schemas.
Use pagination tokens when retrieving large result sets to avoid missing data from API responses.
Verify job completion status before attempting to retrieve artifacts or test metadata to ensure data is available.
antiPatterns: Do not rapidly poll workflow status in quick succession. Use 5-10 second intervals to respect API rate limits.
Do not provide both branch and tag parameters when triggering a pipeline. They are mutually exclusive and will cause an error.
Do not assume job numbers are UUIDs. Job numbers are integers, unlike pipeline and workflow IDs which are UUIDs.

faq.question: What do I need before using this skill?
faq.answer: You need Rube MCP configured in your client and an active CircleCI connection. Use RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS with toolkit 'circleci' to authenticate if not already connected.

faq.question: What is the correct format for project slugs?
faq.answer: Project slugs must include the VCS prefix: 'gh/org/repo' for GitHub repositories or 'bb/org/repo' for Bitbucket. Organization and repository names are case-sensitive.

faq.question: Why can't I retrieve test results for a job?
faq.answer: Test metadata is only available if the job uploaded JUnit XML format results. If no test results were uploaded during job execution, the response will be empty.

faq.question: What is the difference between pipeline IDs, workflow IDs, and job numbers?
faq.answer: Pipeline IDs and workflow IDs are UUIDs (e.g., 5034460f-c7c4-4c43-9457-de07e2029e7b). Job numbers are integers (e.g., 123). Use the correct format for each API endpoint.

faq.question: How do I handle paginated responses?
faq.answer: Check API responses for a 'next_page_token' field. Pass this token as the 'page_token' parameter in your next request. Continue until the token is absent or null.

faq.question: Can this skill modify CircleCI configuration files?
faq.answer: No. This skill can only read data and trigger pipelines through the CircleCI API. It cannot create, edit, or delete .circleci/config.yml files or other repository content.
